input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI Shell app has these problems:
1. GoToAsync("productdetails") throws an exception
2. GoToAsync("//products/active") navigates but nothing visible changes
3. My app takes 3 seconds to start and I have 12 pages in Shell
What's likely wrong in each case and how do I fix them?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI Shell app has these problems:
1. GoToAsync("productdetails") throws an exception
2. GoToAsync("//products/active") navigates but nothing visible changes
3. My app takes 3 seconds to start and I have 12 pages in Shell
What's likely wrong in each case and how do I fix them?

data.content:
Here are the likely causes and fixes:

**1. `GoToAsync("productdetails")` throws an exception**

The route isn't registered. Non-visual (detail) routes must be explicitly registered:

```csharp
// In AppShell constructor or MauiProgram.cs
Routing.RegisterRoute("productdetails", typeof(ProductDetailsPage));
```

Then navigate with:
```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync("productdetails");
```

Only routes defined inline in XAML (`ShellContent`, `Tab`, `FlyoutItem`) are auto-registered. Anything pushed onto the navigation stack needs `Routing.RegisterRoute`.

---

**2. `GoToAsync("//products/active")` navigates but nothing visible changes**

The `//` prefix is an absolute route, meaning Shell switches to the matching `ShellContent`. If the visual tree already shows that content, nothing appears to change. Common causes:

- **Duplicate route names** — two `ShellContent` items resolve to the same route, and Shell switches to the wrong (already-visible) one.
- **Missing `Route` properties** — Shell auto-generates route names from the `Title`, which may not match what you expect.

Fix: explicitly set `Route` on every `ShellContent`, `Tab`, and `FlyoutItem`:

```xml
<FlyoutItem Route="products">
    <Tab Route="active">
        <ShellContent Route="list"
                      Title="Active Products"
                      ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate local:ActiveProductsPage}" />
    </Tab>
</FlyoutItem>
```

Debug by printing registered routes:
```csharp
// Inspect the visual route hierarchy
foreach (var item in Shell.Current.Items)
    System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine(item.Route);
```

---

**3. App takes 3 seconds to start with 12 pages**

Shell instantiates every page whose content is set directly. Use **`DataTemplate`** to defer creation:

```xml
<!-- ❌ Bad — page created at startup -->
<ShellContent Title="Settings">
    <local:SettingsPage />
</ShellContent>

<!-- ✅ Good — page created on first navigation -->
<ShellContent Title="Settings"
              ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate local:SettingsPage}" />
```

Apply `ContentTemplate` to all 12 `ShellContent` elements. This defers page construction until the user actually navigates there, dramatically reducing startup time.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking about three common .NET MAUI Shell navigation issues. These are well-known patterns — I can answer from knowledge without needing to search a codebase.
